items.title: Anthropic addresses Claude Code quality regressions; Fortune summarizes the fallout
items.why_it_matters: Claude Code is a flagship coding agent; reliability regressions (and how they’re handled) directly affect trust and adoption in production dev workflows.
items.brief: Anthropic admits Claude Code got worse; outlines causes/fixes (via postmortem, covered by Fortune).
items.details: Fortune reports on Anthropic’s explanation for a period of degraded Claude Code performance after sustained user complaints. The underlying Anthropic engineering postmortem attributes issues to multiple changes and describes mitigations and reversions. The key takeaway is operational: coding agents need product-level quality gates and regression detection, not just model improvements. If you deploy agent tooling internally, this is a reminder to add evals/monitoring around “agent behavior,” not only model outputs.
